Flash restore is now a one-click **`.zip` download**, and backups can be **replicated off-site** automatically. Thanks again to **@manilx** for the testing and the requests that drove both.

## Added
- **Flash restore as a `.zip` download.** Picking a flash snapshot now streams it straight to your browser as `flash-<id>.zip` (via `restic dump`), ready to drop into the Unraid USB creator or unzip onto a fresh USB. The live `/boot` is never touched. Because a zip carries no filesystem ownership/permission metadata, this also **fixes the "restore failed: There were N errors"** you could hit when the old extract-to-folder restore tried to set Linux permissions on a `/mnt/user` (FUSE) share — the data was fine, but it looked like a failure.
- **Off-site copy (local + remote).** A new optional second repo per domain (Settings → Off-site copy). After each successful local backup, BombVault replicates the new snapshot there with `restic copy`. The local repo stays primary, and replication is best-effort — an off-site hiccup is logged but never fails the local backup. Works with any restic backend (`rest:`, `s3:`, `b2:`, `sftp:`); credentials come from Settings → Cloud credentials.
- **Clearer restore errors.** When restic reports a count-only summary ("There were N errors"), BombVault now appends the first concrete per-item cause so you can see *why*, not just *how many*.

## Fixed
- Saving settings no longer wipes stored **cloud credentials** (the encrypted Cloud-credentials blob was not preserved across a settings save).

## Notes
- Flash restore over HTTP carries the file in the browser; the download endpoint is behind the same authentication as the rest of the app.
- Store migrations v13–v15 add the per-domain off-site repo columns (applied automatically on first start).

Image: `ghcr.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.3.0` (also `:2`, `:2.3`, `:latest`) and `docker.io/junkerderprovinz/bombvault:2.3.0`, multi-arch (amd64 + arm64).
